parts.title: Part 1 - Introduction and Interview
questions: Let's talk about where you live. Do you live in a house or an apartment?
What do you like most about your neighborhood?
How long have you lived there?
What kind of changes would you like to see in your area?
Now let's talk about technology. How often do you use a computer?
What do you mainly use your computer or smartphone for?
Do you think technology has made our lives easier? Why or why not?
What new technology would you like to learn about?

parts.title: Part 2 - Long Turn (Cue Card)
parts.cueCard: Describe an environmental problem in your country. You should say: what the problem is, what causes it, what effects it has, and explain what can be done to solve this problem.
prompts: What can be done to solve it

parts.title: Part 3 - Discussion
questions: What are the main environmental problems facing the world today?
Do you think individuals can make a difference in protecting the environment?
Should governments do more to address environmental issues? In what ways?
How has technology impacted the environment, both positively and negatively?
Do you think future generations will face more serious environmental challenges?
What role should businesses play in environmental protection?
